mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >50% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and residential fixed investment indicate a housing sector downturn that drags on aggregate demand, depressing corporate revenues and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or the joint signal explains less than 2% of return variance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sustained industrial production index above 110 reflects robust capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: IC drops below 0.05 when INDPRO exceeds 110 outside recovery regimes, or 20d forward returns show no statistical difference between high and low production periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative GDP growth quarters, or the signal fails to predict returns in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month acceleration in the consumer price index forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d returns are positive in >40% of instances following CPI acceleration, or the signal produces negative predictive power when inflation is below 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly payroll growth exceeding 100,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict returns when payroll growth exceeds 100k but unemployment rises, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during confirmed labor market improvements.

mechanism_narrative: In mid-cycle expansion regimes, real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized combined with stable inflation confirms sustainable corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns turn negative when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% but CPI accelerates above 3.0%, or the joint signal explains less than 3% of return variance in out-of-sample testing.
